alignments.preprint_claim_text: TLP constitutes the first hardware proposal targeting both off-chip prediction and prefetch filtering using a multi-level perceptron hardware approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TLP constitutes the first hardware proposal targeting both off-chip prediction and prefetch filtering using a multilevel perceptron hardware approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TLP only requires 7KB of storage. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TLP only requires 7KB of storage. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Storage requirement claim is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TLP is effective independently of the L1D prefetching logic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TLP is effective independently of the L1D prefetching logic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim about independence from L1D prefetching logic is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-stage framework effectively transfers mathematical expertise from large to tiny language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed two-stage framework effectively transfers mathematical expertise from large to tiny language models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim verbatim with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates significantly improved performance on the Math23K and Weak12K datasets compared to existing small model methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates significantly improved performance on the Math23K and Weak12K datasets compared to existing small model methods.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework maintains a much lower computational cost than ChatGPT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework maintains a much lower computational cost than ChatGPT.
alignments.rationale: The computational-cost claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Knowledge distilling method cannot guarantee the full utilization of all data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Knowledge distilling method cannot guarantee the full utilization of all data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Toxicity scoring may render pertinent information inaccessible and rigidify cultural norms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Toxicity scoring may render pertinent information inaccessible and prevent language reclamation processes for marginalized people.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the harm to explicitly reference language reclamation for marginalized groups (narrower and more specific) and lowers certainty.

alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same functionality but restates it more directly as a provided mechanism; the scope and confidence are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A pilot study supports the potential of the proposed recourse mechanism, indicating improvements in usability compared to fixed-threshold toxicity-filtering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A pilot study supports the potential of our proposed recourse mechanism, indicating improvements in usability compared to fixed-threshold toxicity-filtering of model outputs.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved but the publication reports it with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work should explore the intersection of toxicity scoring, model controllability, user agency, and language reclamation processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future work should explore the intersection of toxicity scoring, model controllability, user agency, and language reclamation processes.
alignments.rationale: The suggested directions for future work are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using multimodal information yields relative equal-error-rate improvements over text-only and audio-only models of up to 39% and 61%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using multimodal information yields relative equal-error-rate improvements over text-only and audio-only models of up to 39% and 61%.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the size of the LLM and training with low-rank adaption leads to further relative EER reductions of up to 18% on our dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing the size of the LLM and training with low-rank adaption leads to further relative EER reductions of up to 18% on our dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps this experimental result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is feasible to drop the requirement that users must begin each command with a trigger phrase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is feasible to drop the requirement that users must begin each command with a trigger phrase.
alignments.rationale: The feasibility conclusion is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dyslexia significantly impairs different cognitive domains, negatively affecting dyslexic students during their learning path.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this general claim about dyslexia's cognitive impacts; it focuses on recommendation-system results instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recommendation system is highly effective in suggesting the optimal help tools and strategies for dyslexic students.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A recommendation system is highly effective in suggesting the optimal help tools/strategies for dyslexic students, with an error less than 12%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can be used as a new and effective methodology to support students with dyslexia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dyslexic students who used the recommendation algorithm increased their academic scores by almost 1 point on a 1 to 10 scale.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete effectiveness outcome (score increase), which clarifies and substantiates the preprint's broader methodological claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The precision of the implemented recommendation system was 0.85 and its recall was 0.83.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces specific precision/recall metrics that were not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid recommendation model performed best when Pearson’s correlation was used to measure the distance among users and/or items.
alignments.rationale: This methodological/detail result on the best-performing hybrid model and metric is new in the publication and not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian optimization and the hyperband method perform best among the NAS strategies for financial time series forecasting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian optimization and the hyperband method perform best among the NAS strategies for financial time series forecasting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNN and 1D CNN are the best performing architectures among those tested for financial time series forecasting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chain-structured search spaces are the simplest and most applicable to small datasets like time series.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chain-structured search spaces are the simplest and most applicable for small datasets like time series.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed implementation framework for an autonomous, machine learning-based manipulator incorporates human-in-the-loop principles and leverages Extended Reality (XR).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed implementation framework for an autonomous, machine learning-based manipulator incorporates human-in-the-loop principles and leverages Extended Reality (XR) for intuitive communication and programming.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a clarification about the purpose of XR (intuitive communication and programming) but preserves the original claim about an HIL+XR framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human involvement in the robot learning process results in higher adaptability and task generalization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human involvement in the robot learning process results in higher adaptability and task generalization.
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same semantically, but the reported certainty in the publication is lower (medium vs high), indicating tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper emphasizes the importance of developing the digital ecosystem as a whole to support the proposed framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper emphasizes the importance of developing the digital ecosystem as a whole to support the proposed framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review of existing implementation approaches of XR in human-robot collaboration showcases diverse perspectives and methodologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review of existing implementation approaches of XR in human-robot collaboration showcases diverse perspectives and methodologies.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper establishes a user credit evaluation model for communication operators using the fusion LightGBM algorithm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key features related to user evaluation are extracted through data preprocessing and feature engineering methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key features related to user evaluation are extracted from massive data provided by communication operators.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple machine learning algorithms, including linear regression, decision tree, and LightGBM, are utilized to identify the best basic model for user credit evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple machine learning algorithms, including linear regression, decision tree, and LightGBM, are utilized to identify the best basic model for user credit evaluation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study integrates various algorithms such as Averaging, Voting, Blending, and Stacking to refine multiple fusion models for user evaluation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method combines few-shot and active learning with chain-of-thought reasoning for automated assessment.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MVSplat predicts clean feed-forward 3D Gaussians from sparse multi-view images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of ChatGPT raises several concerns, including ethical, social, and employment challenges. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

(certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method is effective. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Expert users, particularly clinicians, can enhance productivity by leveraging LLMs while maintaining critical oversight to identify and correct inaccuracies in AI-generated content. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of LLMs in healthcare poses significant challenges that require proactive dialogue and strategic measures for safe and effective use. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Determining the gravitational theory that gives rise to a prescribed equation of state is a challenging, inverse problem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We successfully apply the algorithm to theories with crossovers, first- and second-order phase transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method holds significant potential for real-time multiple object tracking applications.

alignments.rationale: The publication frames the technical integration as a proof-of-concept application; the specific architectural detail is summarized into a broader contribution statement with increased certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The holistic approach of the system simulates and facilitates a dermatologist's workflow, enhancing dermatological analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mimicking a medical professional's diagnostic process may enhance decision-making in dermatology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about simulating diagnostic workflow but couches it as a may/possible benefit, slightly reducing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology is expected to enhance remote consultation capabilities and access to care in underserved areas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the expectation regarding remote consultation or access-to-care benefits found in the preprint; this claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: No single technology is sufficient on its own for efficient use by dermatologists. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a limitation about technology sufficiency that was not stated among preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed data-driven, modular-based approach outperforms the baseline by an average of 10.6% in the efficiency metric, Success weighted by Path Length (SPL). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework surpasses the baseline by an average of 10.6% in the Success-weighted by Path Length (SPL) efficiency metric. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result vs. baseline and the exact numeric improvement are preserved identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework utilizes a multi-channel Swin-Unet architecture to conduct multi-task learning with multimodal inputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims emphasize a modular map-based approach using LLM-extracted object-to-room knowledge rather than the Swin-Unet multi-channel architecture described in the preprint; the specific architecture claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The real-world demonstration indicates that the proposed approach can efficiently conduct object-goal navigation by traversing several rooms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Real-world demonstrations confirm that the method can effectively navigate multiple rooms in the object-goal navigation task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the real-world navigation result and states it with higher certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced data-driven and modular map-based approach enhances the efficiency of object-goal navigation by utilizing common-sense knowledge of object-to-room relationships extracted from a Large Language Model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a contribution emphasizing use of LLM-extracted object-to-room knowledge and a modular map-based design that did not appear in the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm CAG improves the estimation accuracy of causal graphs when the sample size is small relative to the number of variables and the model is sparse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of conventional causal learning algorithms decreases when the sample size is small relative to the number of variables.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the same issue about accuracy degrading when sample size is small; the preprint's CAG improvement is reflected in the publication context (same scope and certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAG outperforms the original DirectLiNGAM and other divide-and-conquer approaches in both estimation accuracy and computation time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed causal ancestral-relationship-based grouping (CAG) algorithm outperforms the original DirectLiNGAM and other divide-and-conquer approaches in both estimation accuracy and computation time.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim verbatim about CAG's superior accuracy and runtime, so meaning and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The time complexity of the ancestor finding in CAG is cubic to the number of variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The time complexity of the ancestor finding in the CAG algorithm is cubic in the number of variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same complexity claim (cubic) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for causal structure learning are either computationally expensive or do not provide sufficient accuracy when the sample size is small.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing causal discovery methods are computationally expensive or lack sufficient accuracy when the sample size is small.
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same limitation of existing methods under small-sample regimes with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research landscape for writing assistants is increasingly fragmented across various research communities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research landscape for writing assistants is increasingly fragmented across various research communities.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint conclusion about fragmentation with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A design space is proposed as a structured way to examine and explore the multidimensional space of intelligent and interactive writing assistants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A design space is proposed as a structured way to examine and explore the multidimensional space of intelligent and interactive writing assistants.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution (a proposed design space) is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Five aspects of writing assistants—task, user, technology, interaction, and ecosystem—are explored through community collaboration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design space defines dimensions and codes by systematically reviewing 115 papers and leveraging the expertise of researchers in various disciplines.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more detail on how the design space dimensions were defined (systematic review and expert input), clarifying the preprint's statement and increasing methodological specificity and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design space aims to provide a practical tool for researchers and designers to navigate and compare various possibilities of writing assistants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design space aims to offer researchers and designers a practical tool to navigate, comprehend, and compare the various possibilities of writing assistants.
alignments.rationale: Both formulations state the same aim to provide a practical tool for researchers and designers with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Effective peer conversations can significantly enhance the quality of children's collaborative interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Effective peer conversations can significantly enhance the quality of children’s collaborative interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Peer agents manage discussions effectively as team moderators but sometimes have their instructions disregarded.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Peer agents manage discussions effectively as team moderators but sometimes have their instructions disregarded.
alignments.rationale: The result about moderator behavior is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: As participants, peer agents foster children's creative thinking but may not consistently provide timely feedback.
alignments.pub_claim_text: As participants, peer agents foster children’s creative thinking but may not consistently provide timely feedback.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about peer agents as participants.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight potential design improvements and considerations for peer agents in both roles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight potential design improvements and considerations for peer agents in both roles.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about design implications is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a warm start with previously trained networks can significantly reduce training effort in neuroevolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on stitching and layer selection and does not mention warm starts or reduced training effort; the claim appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recombining trained networks is challenging due to differences in architectures and feature representations.
alignments.rationale: The published version does not explicitly state this limitation about architectural and representation differences, so the preprint limitation is not reflected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method of matching layers based on connectivity and employing stitching allows for effective merging of networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach allows for the creation of new networks by selecting which stitching layers to use, enabling the selection of a subnetwork.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same technical idea in terms of selecting stitching layers to create subnetworks, providing a clearer description of the method described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables the creation of new networks that can achieve novel trade-offs between performance and computational cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method finds networks that achieve novel trade-offs between performance and computational cost compared to classical ensembles.
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim about discovering novel performance vs. cost trade-offs is preserved and restated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some newly created networks can outperform the original networks in terms of performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some new networks created through this method even dominate the performance of the original networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (language like "dominate") and raises certainty, indicating amplified confidence that some generated networks outperform originals.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Employing stitching introduces new layers at crossover points to create a new network from two basis networks.
alignments.rationale: This explicit description of stitching (introducing new layers at crossovers) does not have a direct counterpart in the preprint and appears as an added, more explicit formulation in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PECI-Net exhibited higher performance than four recently developed baseline models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PECI-Net exhibited higher performance than four recently developed baseline models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance superiority claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PECI-Net outperformed TernausNet, the best among the baseline models, by 4.54% and UNet by 10.83%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PECI-Net outperformed TernausNet by 4.54% and UNet by 10.83%.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance comparisons are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the ablation studies confirm that CIN and PEN are effective in improving bolus segmentation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the ablation studies confirm that CIN and PEN are effective in improving bolus segmentation performance.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the ablation results showing CIN and PEN effectiveness; wording change to 'conclusion' does not alter the substance or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UMF model significantly outperforms previous baselines in challenging aliased environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The UMF model significantly outperforms previous baselines in challenging aliased environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative/result claim verbatim with identical certainty and scope, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMF leverages multi-modality by using cross-attention blocks between vision and LiDAR features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMF leverages multi-modality by using cross-attention blocks between vision and LiDAR features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication, preserving meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of a re-ranking stage in UMF improves the ordering of top-k candidates based on local feature matching. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of a re-ranking stage in UMF improves the ordering of top-k candidates based on local feature matching. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same description and certainty for the re-ranking-stage contribution, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of UMF was also explored on the RobotCar dataset for broader applicability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of UMF was also explored on the RobotCar dataset for broader applicability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exploratory evaluation claim with the same scope and certainty, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating machine learning into Automated Control Systems enhances decision-making in industrial process management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating machine learning into Automated Control Systems enhances decision-making in industrial process management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The high-level contribution is carried over verbatim in the publication with no added qualifiers or restrictions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the strong vulnerability of models to adversarial samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the strong vulnerability of models to adversarial samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The vulnerability finding is presented identically in the publication and retains the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of defense strategies against adversarial attacks varies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of defense strategies against adversarial attacks varies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions express the same empirical observation about variable defense effectiveness with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel protection approach combining multiple defense methods is proposed and its efficacy is demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new defense strategy based on combining adversarial training and data quantization is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the particular defenses (adversarial training and data quantization) rather than the preprint's generic "multiple defense methods," making the claim more specific (narrower) while preserving high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research contributes several insights into securing machine learning within Automated Control Systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research contributes several insights into securing machine learning within Automated Control Systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement remains the same in content but the publication reports it with higher certainty, indicating increased confidence in the findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A lightweight, domain-adapted pretrained model, prompted with just 20 shots, outperforms a traditional classification model by 30.5% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A lightweight, domain-adapted pretrained language model, prompted with just 20 shots per section class, outperforms a traditional classification model, increasing accuracy from 48.6% to 79.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides exact per-class shot wording and numeric accuracy bounds, clarifying the preprint's improvement claim without changing its substance or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent advances in domain-adaptation and prompting methods showed promising results with minimal training data using lightweight masked language models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying prompting based on general-language pretrained masked language models combined with further-pretraining on medical-domain data achieves significant improvements in accuracy beyond traditional models with minimal training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the general observation as a specific, validated approach (general pretraining + further medical pretraining) with stronger reported results and higher certainty, narrowing and amplifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We are first to present a systematic evaluation of these methods in a low-resource setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about being the first systematic evaluation appears in the publication; the paper drops or reframes the explicit novelty assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We conduct extensive class-wise evaluations supported by Shapley values to validate the quality of our small training data set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using Shapley values for model selection and training data optimization can further increase accuracy up to 84.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports concrete benefits and numeric gains from the Shapley-based evaluations described in the preprint, making the result more explicit and emphasizing improved accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the feasibility of deploying powerful machine learning methods in clinical settings and can serve as a process-oriented guideline for lower-resource languages and domains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This higher-level deployment/guideline framing does not appear in the preprint claims list and is presented as a new concluding implication in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sequential format called Linearized MusicXML is defined to train an end-to-end model while maintaining compatibility with the MusicXML format. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We define a sequential format called Linearized MusicXML, allowing us to train an end-to-end model directly and maintain close cohesion and compatibility with the industry-standard MusicXML format. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with very similar wording and intent; no substantive change is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dev and test set for benchmarking typeset OMR with MusicXML ground truth is created based on the OpenScore Lieder corpus. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We create a dev and test set for benchmarking typeset OMR based on OpenScore Lieder corpus, containing 1,438 and 1,493 pianoform systems, each with an image from IMSLP and MusicXML ground truth. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds concrete dataset sizes and sources, clarifying but not altering the claim's scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An end-to-end model is trained and fine-tuned to serve as a baseline on the dataset, evaluated using the TEDn metric. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We train and fine-tune an end-to-end model to serve as a baseline on the dataset and employ the TEDn metric to evaluate the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The baseline-model training and TEDn evaluation are described equivalently in the publication, preserving claim meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model surpasses the state-of-the-art results when tested against the synthetic pianoform dataset GrandStaff. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We test our model against the recently published synthetic pianoform dataset GrandStaff and surpass the state-of-the-art results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result claim about outperforming prior methods on GrandStaff with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HierCode facilitates zero-shot recognition of Out-Of-Vocabulary (OOV) characters by utilizing shared radicals and structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HierCode facilitates zero-shot recognition of Out-Of-Vocabulary (OOV) characters by utilizing shared radicals and structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The zero-shot mechanism and its purpose are stated identically in both versions, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HierCode excels in line-level recognition tasks by computing similarity with visual features, providing a notable advantage over existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HierCode excels in line-level recognition tasks by computing similarity with visual features, providing a notable advantage over existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The line-level recognition advantage is preserved verbatim in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments have showcased HierCode's superiority for both conventional and zero-shot Chinese character or text recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments have showcased HierCode’s superiority for both conventional and zero-shot Chinese character or text recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HierCode exhibits state-of-the-art performance with significantly fewer parameters and fast inference speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HierCode exhibits state-of-the-art performance with significantly fewer parameters and lower floating-point operations (FLOPs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the informal "fast inference speed" with a specific efficiency metric (lower FLOPs), clarifying the efficiency claim without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent progress in X-ray focusing optics has developed ARPES into a microscopic tool for spatially mapping the electronic structure of solids. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent progress in x-ray focusing optics has developed ARPES into a microscopic tool for spatially mapping the electronic structure of solids. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with essentially identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implemented protocol autonomously searches both $\f{k}$- and real space to identify positions of interest based on photoemission intensity or sharp spectral features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implemented protocol autonomously searches both k- and real-space to find positions of particular interest based on photoemission intensity or sharp spectral features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is the same as the preprint claim with only minor formatting/wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The search protocol utilizes Gaussian process regression and can be expanded to include additional parameters or optimization criteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The search protocol utilizes Gaussian process regression and can be expanded to include additional parameters or optimization criteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The method and its extensibility are described identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PyVRP is a Python package that implements hybrid genetic search in a state-of-the-art vehicle routing problem (VRP) solver. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PyVRP combines the flexibility of Python with the performance of C++. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes implementation details (Python/C++ tradeoff) not present in the preprint; this reframes the package description rather than contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PyVRP ranked 1st in the 2021 DIMACS VRPTW challenge and, after improvements, ranked 1st on the static variant of the EURO meets NeurIPS 2022 vehicle routing competition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PyVRP ranked first in the 2021 DIMACS VRPTW challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PyVRP achieves state-of-the-art results on the VRPTW and capacitated VRP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PyVRP achieves state-of-the-art results on the VRPTW and capacitated VRP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PyVRP is designed for the VRP with time windows (VRPTW) but can be easily extended to support other VRP variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about extensibility to other VRP variants appears in the publication; the related publication claim instead focuses on implementation language/performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed High-order Vision Mamba UNet (H-vmunet) enhances the adaptability of 2D-selective-scan (SS2D) for medical image segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim enhanced ‘adaptability’ of SS2D; its claims focus on module-level improvements and empirical performance instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The High-order 2D-selective-scan (H-SS2D) reduces the introduction of redundant information during SS2D operations through higher-order interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The High-order 2D-selective-scan (H-SS2D) module reduces the introduction of redundant information during feature learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same technical effect with slightly altered wording (feature learning vs SS2D operations), clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Local-SS2D module improves the learning ability of local features of SS2D at each order of interaction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Local-SS2D module improves the learning ability of local features in the SS2D framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim with minor wording changes and emphasis on the framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparison and ablation experiments demonstrate the strong competitiveness of H-vmunet in medical image segmentation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The H-vmunet model demonstrates strong performance in medical image segmentation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical performance claim is preserved between preprint and publication with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants in the training events show about 60% completion of the 11-week training course. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 60% of participants complete the 11-week training course. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same completion-rate result with equivalent phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hackathon results from two pilot studies indicate increasing expertise among participants, as evidenced by progression in the achieved points on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hackathon results from two pilot studies show increasing expertise among participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding about increasing participant expertise from the hackathons.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 70% of participants positively evaluate the use of the gamification approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More than 70% of participants positively evaluate the use of the gamification approach. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 85% of participants consider the implemented Use Cases suitable for their investigations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More than 85% of participants consider the implemented use cases suitable for their investigations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A large portion of the evaluation results expressed dramatically inflated Type I errors, especially at privacy budget levels of ε≤1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large portion of the evaluation results expressed dramatically inflated Type I errors, especially at levels of ϵ ≤ 1. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low p-values may be obtained in statistical tests simply as a byproduct of the noise added to protect privacy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low p-values may be obtained in statistical tests simply as a byproduct of the noise added to protect privacy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A DP smoothed histogram-based synthetic data generation method was shown to produce valid Type I error for all privacy levels tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A DP Smoothed Histogram-based synthetic data generation method was shown to produce valid Type I error for all privacy levels tested. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The DP Smoothed Histogram-based method required a large original dataset size and a modest privacy budget (ϵ ≥ 5) in order to have reasonable Type II error levels. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical Adam algorithm is a first-order implicit-explicit (IMEX) Euler discretization of the underlying ordinary differential equation (ODE). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical ADAM algorithm is a first-order implicit-explicit (IMEX) Euler discretization of the underlying ordinary differential equation (ODE). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: New extensions of the ADAM scheme can be obtained by using higher-order IMEX methods to solve the ODE. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new optimization algorithm derived from this approach performs better than classical Adam on several regression and classification problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new optimization algorithm derived from this approach performs better than classical ADAM on several regression and classification problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed enhanced lightweight YOLOv5 technique achieves a 1% increase in detection accuracy compared to the existing YOLOv5. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed enhanced lightweight YOLOv5 technique achieves a 1% increase in detection accuracy compared to the existing YOLOv5. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in claim content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of the C3Ghost module reduces floating point operations per second (FLOPs) by 13% in the feature channel fusion process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of the C3Ghost module reduces floating point operations per second (FLOPs) by 13% in the feature channel fusion process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about FLOPs reduction and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FasterNet module improves feature extraction efficiency and reduces computational overhead by processing only a portion of the input channels. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Edge deployment of proprietary large language models introduces new security challenges, particularly model stealing attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Edge deployment of proprietary large language models introduces new security challenges, particularly model stealing attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level conclusion with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing defense mechanisms fail to provide effective protection against model stealing attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing defense mechanisms fail to provide effective protection against model stealing attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion without substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TransLinkGuard is proposed as a plug-and-play model protection approach that addresses the security challenges of edge-deployed models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransLinkGuard is proposed as a plug-and-play model protection approach that addresses the security challenges of edge-deployed models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented the same way in the publication as in the preprint, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TransLinkGuard achieves the same security protection as black-box security guarantees with negligible runtime overhead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransLinkGuard achieves the same security as black-box guarantees with negligible runtime overhead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance and overhead claim with equivalent certainty; wording differences are minor and do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models (DDIM) struggle with reproducing radiometric features of large-scale multi-patch imagery, resulting in inconsistencies across the full image. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Denoising diffusion implicit models (DDIM) struggle with reproducing radiometric features of large-scale multipatch imagery, resulting in inconsistencies across the full image. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint; no change in scope or confidence is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed method leverages denoising diffusion for effective multi-sensor optical image translation over large areas, super-resolving large-scale low spatial resolution images into high-resolution equivalents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed method leverages denoising diffusion for effective multisensor optical image translation over large areas, super-resolving large-scale low spatial resolution images into high-resolution equivalents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement in the publication matches the preprint wording and claim content closely, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach provides precise domain adaptation, preserving image content while improving radiometric accuracy and feature representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach provides precise domain adaptation, preserving image content while improving radiometric accuracy and feature representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental-result claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication, indicating no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We reach a mean Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (mLPIPS) of 0.1884 and a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of 45.64, outperforming all compared methods, including DDIM, ShuffleMixer, and SwinIR. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We reach a mean learned perceptual image patch similarity of 0.1884 and a Frchet Inception Distance of 45.64, expressively outperforming all compared methods, including DDIM, ShuffleMixer, and SwinIR. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical results and comparative claim are the same between preprint and publication (minor orthographic differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usefulness of our approach is further demonstrated in two Heterogeneous Change Detection tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usefulness of our approach is further demonstrated in two Heterogeneous Change Detection tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding demonstration claim appears verbatim and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model introduces an explicit motion model as a factorized 4D representation that is fast and exploits the spatio-temporal correlation of the motion field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model introduces an explicit motion model as a factorized 4D representation that is fast and exploits the spatio-temporal correlation of the motion field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution statement is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization with sparse input viewpoints necessitates the use of motion priors to constrain the learning process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model utilizes reliable flow priors, including a combination of sparse flow priors across cameras and dense flow priors within cameras, to regularize the motion model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the general necessity claim with a more specific description of the priors used (sparse across cameras, dense within cameras) and states it more confidently as a utilized regularizer, narrowing and amplifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves very good performance on popular multi-view dynamic scene datasets with sparse input viewpoints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model achieves very good performance on popular multi-view dynamic scene datasets with sparse input viewpoints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance result is reproduced with equivalent wording and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing fast dynamic scene models do not explicitly model the motion, making them difficult to be constrained with motion priors. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation about other fast dynamic scene models is not present in the publication and appears to have been removed or omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Structured Neuron-level Pruning (SNP) effectively compresses and accelerates Transformer-based models for both edge devices and server processors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structured Neuron-level Pruning (SNP) effectively compresses and accelerates Transformer-based models for both edge devices and server processors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeiT-Small with SNP runs 3.1 times faster than the original model and achieves performance that is 21.94% faster and 1.12% higher than the DeiT-Tiny. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeiT-Small with SNP runs 3.1 times faster than the original model and achieves performance that is 21.94% faster and 1.12% higher than the DeiT-Tiny. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical speed and performance comparisons are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNP with head pruning could compress the DeiT-Base by 80% of the parameters and computational costs and achieve 3.85 times faster inference speed on RTX3090 and 4.93 times on Jetson Nano. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific DeiT-Base compression and the exact speedups on the RTX3090/Jetson Nano are not present in the publication; instead a different acceleration result (on EfficientFormer) appears.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional pruning approaches can only compress and accelerate the MSA module using head pruning, although the head is not an atomic unit. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This critical observation about conventional pruning approaches does not appear in the publication and seems omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SNP accelerates the EfficientFormer model by 1.74 times on the Jetson Nano with acceptable performance degradation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new specific result (EfficientFormer acceleration on Jetson Nano) that was not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prompt Optimizer for Abstract Concepts (POAC) significantly improves the accuracy and aesthetic quality of generated images from abstract concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Prompt Optimizer for Abstract Concepts (POAC) significantly improves the accuracy and aesthetic quality of generated images from abstract concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Prompt Language Model (PLM) is fine-tuned with a curated dataset of abstract concept prompts to enhance text-to-image diffusion models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Prompt Language Model (PLM) is fine-tuned with a curated dataset of abstract concept prompts to enhance text-to-image diffusion models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework employs a Reinforcement Learning-based optimization strategy to align generated images with optimized prompts. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel symbolic Machine Learning can rediscover traditional nuclear physics models or identify alternatives with improved simplicity, fidelity, and predictive power. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Symbolic Machine Learning can rediscover traditional nuclear physics models or identify alternatives with improved simplicity, fidelity, and predictive power. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
